title: Mock workers
description: Run you-agent-factory with deterministic mock worker outcomes via --with-mock-workers instead of live provider dispatch.
openingSummary: Mock workers replace live worker dispatch with deterministic mock outcomes so you can validate routing and failure paths without live provider calls.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Mock workers is the you-agent-factory Program documentation surface for deterministic mock-worker runs. Use it when you need to understand --with-mock-workers and how mock outcomes differ from ordinary live worker dispatch. This page is not a live worker taxonomy, not a record/replay guide, and not Model Atlas material.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: Mock-worker mode replaces live worker dispatch with deterministic mock outcomes for a you run. Pass --with-mock-workers to enable that mode. An optional JSON path selects mock entries; omitting the path enables mock mode with an empty config. Mock workers are distinct from live workers and from record/replay artifact reuse.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Enable mock-worker mode on you run with --with-mock-workers, optionally pointing at a mock-workers JSON config. Later teaching on this page will cover the JSON selection contract, runType outcomes, and unmatchedDispatchPolicy. Start from the flag itself when you need a deterministic run without live provider calls.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web mock-workers reference for you-agent-factory. It is not a live workers taxonomy, not a resources capacity deep-dive, not a record/replay guide, and not a sync of packaged CLI docs. Scaffold sections identify the surface; detailed contract teaching, examples, and operational cautions land in follow-on authoring.
